framing_signals_checked: Does the engineering layer claim the Gita is really a tech manual? No — the analog is presented as additive interpretation, not substitution.
Does it claim Krishna was 'really talking about engineering opportunities'? No — the kṣatriya-specific frame is preserved in the source pack and the engineering layer is named as an analog.

Does it project modern frames backward? It uses modern engineering scenarios but does not claim Sanskrit anticipated them. The doctrinal claim (unsought-arrival as marker) is what generalises, not the kṣatriya-specific instance.
verdict_reason: No tech-manual framing. The verse is treated as an analog, with the original carefully preserved. PASS.
